rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Status Of Stipulated Briefing Schedule For Motions For Summary Judgment/Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Status on stipulated summary judgment briefing schedules: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. The parties should bring to the hearing stipulations regarding summary motion dates. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Campbell Soup Co.¿s motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. Plaintiff¿s request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. 437c(h) is granted. Plaintiff demonstrated that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot, for reasons stated, be presented. The supplemental opposition and supplemental reply dates will be determined at the hearing as well as the new hearing date. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant The Board of Trustees of the Leland Stanford Junior University¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known that the dust and debris to which its employees exposed plaintiff contained asbestos. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known of the health hazards related to asbestos exposure. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Allied Painters, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Namely, plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether the joint compound to which he was exposed by defendant¿s employees contained asbestos. Plaintiff¿s objections to the General Order standard interrogatory responses from Kelly-Moore, Georgia-Pacific and Hamilton Materials are overruled because defendant is entitled to submit the interrogatory responses since plaintiff used the same responses to support his claim against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Rosendahl Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories, which were served prior to questioning by defendant¿s counsel at his deposition, stated that plaintiff has knowledge that the insulation materials and debris with which defendant worked contained asbestos and defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not have such knowledge. Plaintiff represents that he withdraws his strict liability and false representation causes of action and his claim for punitive damages, which were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. Defendant¿s motion was timely. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
